comment: Quality Gates catalog -- docs-repro dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/docs-repro/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/docs-repro/.

asserts: in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container with cargo + python3 + curl pre-installed and the workspace cargo build complete, bash examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh exits 0
the run leaves a partial-clone working tree at /tmp/reposix-example-01
the example pushes a commit and the simulator's audit log shows a helper_push_* row

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh regressed OR the sim/cli/helper changed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver -- real blocker was container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim; it now starts an ephemeral sim on the host and runs the example container with --network host. QL-001 is landed (P91-02); the 'cold container build budget' cause was moot (the gate runs on host with warm binaries). Check examples/01-shell-loop/expected-output.md + target/debug binaries.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the example did not work end to end, bash examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh would exit non-zero OR the simulator's audit log would lack a helper_push_* row; either directly falsifies the 'exits 0 and pushes' claim. The gate now runs the example against a driver-started sim reachable via --network host, so a real failure surfaces as a non-zero container exit.

asserts: in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container, python3 examples/02-python-agent/run.py exits 0
the agent finds 'database' in some issue body and applies a severity label
git push reports success

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/02-python-agent/run.py regressed OR the sim/cli/helper changed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver: real blockers were (a) container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim (now started on host + --network host) and (b) run.py globbing '*.md' at repo root instead of the canonical 'issues/*.md' (QL-001), which matched 0 records yet exited 0 -- a vacuous-GREEN honesty hazard now closed by a fail-loud zero-match guard. QL-001 is landed (P91-02). Keep the python stdlib-only constraint.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the agent did not apply a severity label and push, run.py would exit non-zero (it now fails loud on a zero 'database' match instead of exiting 0 on a no-op) OR 'git push' would report failure; either falsifies the 'finds database + labels + pushes' asserts. The container gate greps the captured stdout for a non-zero match count and a push summary.

asserts: examples/03-claude-code-skill/RUN.md exists and references .claude/skills/reposix-agent-flow
examples/03-claude-code-skill/agent-prompt.md exists and parses as markdown
this row is graded by manual verifier-subagent dispatch (not auto-runnable)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/03-claude-code-skill/{RUN.md, agent-prompt.md} got out of sync with .claude/skills/reposix-agent-flow

asserts: bash examples/04-conflict-resolve/run.sh exits 0 in ubuntu:24.04 container
the second agent observes 'fetch first' from helper stderr and recovers via git pull --rebase
after recovery, both edits are merged

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/04-conflict-resolve/run.sh regressed OR push-time conflict detection regressed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver: real blockers were (a) container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim (now started on host + --network host), (b) an 'ls *.md' glob at repo root instead of canonical 'issues/*.md' (QL-001), (c) agents A and B SHARING one reposix cache -- which masked the conflict (A's push advanced the shared base so B's stale push looked current); fixed by giving each agent its own REPOSIX_CACHE_DIR, and (d) the rebase recovery not deleting the fast-import staging ref refs/reposix-import/main before re-fetch. Push-time conflict detection is NOT regressed (proven once the cache split let it fire). QL-001 is landed (P91-02).
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If conflict recovery did not work, the second (stale-base) agent's push would be accepted instead of rejected (no 'fetch first'), OR the rebase-and-retry would fail to land both edits; either falsifies the conflict-then-recover asserts. With per-agent caches the helper now emits 'local base version: 1, backend version: 2 ... Run: git pull --rebase' and rejects B, then the recovery re-pushes clean.

asserts: bash examples/05-blob-limit-recovery/run.sh exits 0 in ubuntu:24.04 container

run.sh confirms the blob-limit guard constant BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT is committed in crates/reposix-remote/src/stateless_connect.rs (step [1/4], the grep gate at run.sh:28-30 exits non-zero under set -euo pipefail if the constant is absent) and applies the pre-emptive git sparse-checkout partial-clone pattern (steps [3/4]-[4/4], run.sh:45-48/55-56) that narrows the fetch scope under REPOSIX_BLOB_LIMIT=3; it does NOT trigger or read a runtime blob-limit stderr error (the fast-import fetch path bypasses the per-RPC command=fetch check -- see expected-output.md '## Why no blob_limit_exceeded audit row')

after the sparse-checkout-narrowed git checkout of refs/reposix/origin/main, at least one targeted issues/*.md record blob is materialized in the working tree (the bare 'ls issues/*.md' at run.sh:51 and again at run.sh:58 exits non-zero under set -e on a zero-match glob); the check establishes materialization of the narrowed set, not that siblings remain sparse

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/05-blob-limit-recovery/run.sh regressed OR the blob-limit guard constant / sparse-checkout path regressed. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver: real blockers were (a) container-rehearse.sh never starting a reachable sim (now started on host + --network host) and (b) 'git sparse-checkout set 0001.md ...' using the OLD zero-padded flat pathspecs -- canonical is 'issues/1.md' (QL-001, unpadded + bucketed); the 'ls' inspection lines had the same drift. QL-001 is landed (P91-02). HONESTY REWORD (2026-07-13, quick 260713-q0e): asserts #2/#3 were reworded to the TRUTH -- example-05 does NOT observe a runtime blob-limit stderr error (the fast-import fetch path bypasses the per-RPC check; expected-output.md documents zero blob_limit_exceeded rows). It exercises the PRE-EMPTIVE git sparse-checkout pattern + the source-constant presence ONLY. The real runtime-error recovery is covered by quality/gates/agent-ux/dark-factory.sh; a deeper example-05 fix (exercise the real runtime error + recovery cycle) is filed for v0.15.0 (SURPRISES-INTAKE). Do NOT re-introduce a 'observes the error from stderr and recovers' claim here -- run.sh provably never reads helper stderr.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the pre-emptive partial-clone workflow did not hold, run.sh exits non-zero and its asserts land in asserts_failed: the step-[1/4] grep gate (run.sh:28-30) exits 1 under set -euo pipefail if BLOB_LIMIT_EXCEEDED_FMT is absent from stateless_connect.rs, and the bare 'ls issues/*.md' (run.sh:51 and :58) exits non-zero under set -e on a zero-match glob if the sparse-checkout-narrowed checkout materialized no record. The row DELIBERATELY does not claim a runtime blob-limit stderr observation: the fast-import fetch path bypasses the per-RPC command=fetch check (expected-output.md), so example-05 provably exercises the pre-emptive sparse-checkout pattern + source-constant presence only, and the true runtime-error recovery is covered by quality/gates/agent-ux/dark-factory.sh.

sources: (historical: scripts/repro-quickstart.sh -- migrated to this gate by SIMPLIFY-06; see commit history)

asserts: step 2: simulator starts and listens on 127.0.0.1:7878
step 3: reposix init sim::demo /tmp/repo exits 0 and configures partial-clone
step 4: git checkout -B main refs/reposix/origin/main exits 0
step 5: issues/1.md exists with title: frontmatter
step 7: git push reports main -> main
step 8: audit_events_cache has a row with op LIKE 'helper_push_%'

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/tutorials/first-run.md or tutorial-replay.sh drifted. P106 (2026-07-12) RETIRED the stale P90 waiver -- BOTH cited causes were wrong: (1) the 'cold container build budget' was moot because the gate runs ON HOST with warm binaries (verifier.container corrected to null, kind:mechanical), and (2) QL-001 was already landed (P91-02) and push works on git 2.25.1. The REAL bugs were harness-local: the sim bound :7780 while 'reposix init' targets the default :7878 (now bound :7878); step 5 asserted a stale root '0001.md' (canonical is issues/1.md); and step 7 grepped stdout for 'main -> main' though git writes it to STDERR (now greps combined output). Runs hermetically under its own REPOSIX_CACHE_DIR.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the tutorial did not replay clean, one of steps 2-8 (sim up, init exit 0, checkout, issues/1.md materialized with title, push 'main -> main', helper_push_% audit row) would fail and be recorded in asserts_failed; each maps 1:1 to a green-contract assert. The harness now exits 0 with all 7 asserts_passed on a real run.

asserts: docs/benchmarks/latency.md frontmatter last_measured_at is < 30 days old
the cached-read p50 cell value matches the headline 8 ms to within +/-2ms

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/benchmarks/latency.md drifted from headline numbers; v0.12.1 perf cross-check (perf/headline-numbers-cross-check) automates this row D-CONV-2 (2026-07-04): this row is the reason the weekly verdict is permanently yellow, not red-or-green -- intentionally NOT waived/softened (OD-4 SS3: mechanically verifying headline numbers is the launch-readiness milestone's job). Plan: GOOD-TO-HAVES-04 in .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md routes mechanizing this row (real verifier.script, reusing perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py's extraction logic) to the launch-readiness milestone.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the headline 8ms cached-read claim were false, either assert would falsify it: a stale last_measured_at (>=30d) means the reviewer can no longer trust the recorded number and must FAIL pending re-measurement, and a p50 cell value outside 8ms+/-2ms directly contradicts the docs/index.md and README.md headline text the row exists to police.

asserts: headline 89.1% is greppable from docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md
the comparison fixture file referenced from token-economy.md exists

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md drifted from headline numbers; v0.12.1 perf cross-check (perf/headline-numbers-cross-check) automates this row D-CONV-2 (2026-07-04): this row is the reason the weekly verdict is permanently yellow, not red-or-green -- intentionally NOT waived/softened (OD-4 SS3: mechanically verifying headline numbers is the launch-readiness milestone's job). Plan: GOOD-TO-HAVES-04 in .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md routes mechanizing this row (real verifier.script, reusing perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py's extraction logic) to the launch-readiness milestone.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the 89.1% token-reduction headline were false, the reviewer's grep for the exact figure in docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md would come back empty (the number was never actually printed where the headline claims it lives), or the comparison fixture backing the calculation would be missing -- either finding directly falsifies the claim and flips the row to FAIL rather than PASS.

asserts: every fenced code block in user-facing docs has a catalog row in quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible.json or is matched by an allow-list entry
no catalogued snippet drifted from its source (sha256 match)

the allow-list-mode escalation advisory fires on the UNCOVERED block count exceeding PIVOT_THRESHOLD (50), not the raw block count -- an allow-list or catalog entry can retire the advisory (GTH-V15-49 Option B, 2026-07-16)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a doc edit added a fenced code block without a catalog row OR a catalogued row's source content drifted; fix by either pasting the --write-template output or rolling back the doc edit. If the new block is illustrative, add the derived_id to quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible-allowlist.json with a reason. If RED is the '>50 uncovered blocks' escalation advisory: it counts UNCOVERED blocks only (GTH-V15-49 Option B, 2026-07-16) -- cataloguing or allow-listing the individual uncovered blocks (required anyway to clear the itemized failures) also clears this advisory; do NOT bump PIVOT_THRESHOLD.
